scope: Techniques for extracting predictive signals from timestamps, including basic calendar derivations (day of week, hour) and mathematical encodings (sine/cosine) to represent the cyclic nature of time.
key_concepts: Raw timestamps (e.g., UNIX epochs or datetime strings) are rarely useful to models directly and must be parsed into component parts.
Calendar features isolate specific temporal components like 'day of the week', 'is weekend', or 'hour of the day' to capture human behavioral patterns.
Time is inherently cyclic: December (month 12) is temporally adjacent to January (month 1), and 23:00 is close to 01:00.
Linear numeric representations of time fail to capture this adjacency. Sine and cosine transformations map cyclic features onto a circle, preserving the true distance between time periods.

definitions.definition: A technique that uses sine and cosine functions to transform periodic features (like time) into a two-dimensional coordinate system, preserving their circular nature.

examples.description: Extracting an 'is_holiday' boolean feature from a transaction timestamp.
Encoding 'hour of day' (0-23) into two features: sin(2π * hour / 24) and cos(2π * hour / 24).

relations.builds_toward: Temporal data modeling and time-series representations in Chapter 10.

figures.caption: Transformasi Siklik dengan Sinus dan Kosinus
figures.description: Diagram berbentuk jam yang menunjukkan bagaimana nilai jam (0-23) dipetakan ke dalam koordinat lingkaran dua dimensi (sinus dan kosinus) sehingga jam 23 dan jam 0 berada berdekatan.

exclusions: Time-series lag features or rolling windows (saved strictly for Chapter 10).
